metadata.title:
Haywood Defense Has Sensation 10 Spring.


Easter|y Tells Of War on Union Miners.

pages.raw_text:
stopped to warn the boys to get out


II got my Winchester shotgun
and plenty of ammunition and
marched down the street through
Independence. We met a number
of members, some armed, others
not.


1--WE went to the city hall and
could see from there 5 carloads OF
Citizens alliance men and the
militia 60 up to Bull hill. They got
of and gathered up all the union
men. Then we saw them loot a
union store. We got away to Gil.
lette, where l hill "a hours, then
boarded a train and escaped to
Denver, where l reported what
happened"


After wandering about consider.
ably Easterly finally located In Sill.
yer City, Idaho, where he has been
working since. He met Jack Simp.
kins there in December, 1905
Simpkins told him Orchard was
selling mining stock and writing
insurance in southern Idaho. Simp.
kins. he said, came to Silver City
at the request of the union to or.
ganize new local.


Soon after Simpkins went back
Orchard wrote him saying he was
doing fine and wanted him to come
out and Join him selling stock and
writing insurance. On 2 occasions
he had similar letters from Orch.
ard.


Borah then cross examined East
erly, who said he knew Steunen.
berg was opposed to the federation.
Orchard told him In Altman, In
August, 1903, that because Steunen.
berg had deported him he would
get even. Easterly admitted he
knew in November and December
1905, that Orchard was in Caldwell,
but he didn't know Steunenberg
lived there. Asked why he hadn I
given information that Orchard
threatened Steunenberg, Easterly
said:


IL have never been an informer."
Easterly denied that he left an
internal machine under the bed
when he left the district. He did
however, leave 2 rifles there.
Joseph Sholz, a Mullan miner
formerly of Cripple Creek, who was
accused by Orchard of having made
trip into the Vindicator at the
time the attempt was made to
blow up carlosd of powder was
called.


He confronted Orchard on the
stand. Orchard said he was not
the man he meant.


Sholz said the only other sholz
he knew in the district was a man
he understood was a "gun man.
for the mine owners. He was not
cross examined.


George Breen, rancher, formerly
railroad man on the Florence &
Cripple Creek railway, was in In
dependence the day OF the depot
explosion. He saw the hounds
bought from the penitentiary to
the scene Of the crime.
Here court adjourned for recess.
Easterly went on the stand late
yesterday afternoon after James
Maher. former secretary and treas.
urer of the Western Federation,
had corroborated Boyce's state.
ments In regard ta the organization
He was positive that the federation
